skill.name: supabase
skill.description: Use when doing ANY task involving Supabase. Triggers: Supabase products (Database, Auth, Edge Functions, Realtime, Storage, Vectors, Cron, Queues); client libraries and SSR integrations (supabase-js, @supabase/ssr) in Next.js, React, SvelteKit, Astro, Remix; auth issues (login, logout, sessions, JWT, cookies, getSession, getUser, getClaims, RLS); Supabase CLI or MCP server; schema changes, migrations, security audits, Postgres extensions (pg_graphql, pg_cron, pg_vector).
skill.summary: Official Supabase agent skill providing database, auth, storage, and MCP server best practices for AI coding assistants.
skill.author: supabase

security_audit.summary: All 66 static analyzer findings are false positives. The analyzer treated markdown documentation (CLI examples, URLs, and reference links) as executable code. The skill contains only Supabase best-practice documentation, CLI usage guides, and a feedback template. No actual code execution, credential handling, or network operations exist in the skill files. Safe for publication.

content.user_title: Build with Supabase databases and auth
content.value_statement: Supabase changes frequently and has security traps that silently create vulnerabilities. This skill provides verified best practices for schema design, RLS policies, auth security, CLI usage, and MCP server setup.
actual_capabilities: Provide up-to-date Supabase best practices for database schema design and migrations
Configure Row Level Security (RLS) policies with correct access patterns
Guide Supabase CLI usage with version-specific commands and known gotchas
Set up and troubleshoot Supabase MCP server connections
Secure auth flows including JWT handling, session management, and API key usage
Navigate Supabase documentation via MCP search, markdown fetching, or web search
limitations: Relies on external Supabase documentation which may change between skill versions
CLI commands require the Supabase CLI to be installed locally
MCP server features require OAuth authentication and internet connectivity
Does not replace official Supabase support for production incidents

use_cases.title: Secure database schema setup
use_cases.description: Design PostgreSQL schemas with proper RLS policies, avoiding common Supabase security traps like exposed service_role keys and views that bypass row-level security.

use_cases.title: Auth integration debugging
use_cases.description: Resolve authentication issues involving JWT claims, session management, cookie handling, and authorization logic using verified Supabase security patterns.
use_cases.target_user: Developers integrating Supabase Auth with Next.js or React applications

use_cases.title: Migration workflow management
use_cases.description: Create, iterate, and commit database migrations using the correct Supabase CLI workflow, avoiding common pitfalls with migration history and schema diffs.
use_cases.target_user: Backend engineers managing Supabase database version control

prompt_templates.title: Create a table with RLS
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Supabase table called 'projects' in the public schema with Row Level Security enabled. Include policies for authenticated users to read their own projects and admins to manage all projects.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setting up their first Supabase database table

prompt_templates.title: Debug auth session issues
prompt_templates.prompt: My Supabase getSession() returns null after page refresh in Next.js. The auth cookie is set but the server-side call fails. Check my middleware and auth configuration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate developer troubleshooting Supabase SSR authentication

prompt_templates.title: Set up MCP server
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up the Supabase MCP server in my project. Create the .mcp.json configuration file and walk me through the OAuth authentication flow to enable database query tools.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer integrating Supabase MCP tools into their AI coding workflow

prompt_templates.title: Secure storage policies
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure Supabase Storage bucket policies so users can upload and update their own avatar images but cannot access other users' files. Include the required INSERT, SELECT, and UPDATE policies.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced developer implementing fine-grained storage access control

output_examples.input: Create a users table with RLS for a multi-tenant SaaS app
output_examples.output: The skill guides you through creating the table with proper RLS policies, enabling row-level security, setting up org-based access control, and avoiding common pitfalls like using user_metadata for authorization decisions.

output_examples.input: My supabase db diff produces empty output after applying changes
output_examples.output: The skill explains that apply_migration was likely used instead of execute_sql for iteration, and walks through the correct workflow: use execute_sql for iteration, then supabase db pull to generate the final migration file.

best_practices: Enable Row Level Security on every table in exposed schemas and create policies that match the actual access model
Never use user_metadata claims for authorization decisions in RLS policies since they are user-editable
Always verify Supabase features against current documentation before implementing, as APIs and CLI commands change between versions
anti_patterns: Exposing service_role or secret keys in frontend code or NEXT_PUBLIC environment variables
Using apply_migration for iterative schema changes instead of execute_sql or supabase db query
Placing security definer functions in exposed schemas instead of private schemas

faq.question: Does this skill work with the latest Supabase CLI version?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the AI to discover CLI commands via --help and check the version before use. It accounts for version-specific features like db advisors requiring CLI v2.81.3+.

faq.question: How does this skill handle Row Level Security?
faq.answer: It enforces RLS by default on all exposed tables, provides specific guidance on policy creation, and warns about common pitfalls like views bypassing RLS and UPDATE requiring SELECT policies.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Supabase MCP server setup?
faq.answer: Yes, it provides troubleshooting steps for MCP connectivity, .mcp.json configuration, and OAuth 2.1 authentication flow setup.

faq.question: What Supabase products does this skill cover?
faq.answer: Database, Auth, Edge Functions, Realtime, Storage, Vectors, Cron, and Queues. It also covers CLI usage, MCP server integration, and Postgres extensions like pg_graphql, pg_cron, and pg_vector.

faq.question: How do I report incorrect guidance from this skill?
faq.answer: The skill includes a feedback workflow that drafts a GitHub issue using a structured template and submits it to the supabase/agent-skills repository for maintainer review.

faq.question: Is it safe to use the anon key in frontend code?
faq.answer: The anon key is designed for client-side use, but the skill warns that in Next.js any NEXT_PUBLIC environment variable is sent to the browser. Never use service_role or secret keys in public clients.
